notes: Chunk has no fiscal_kpis (null) — all candidates come from event text.

Reviewed all 3 events in order: 10-Q 0001546417-25-000103 (part 2/2, Financial Condition + Risk Factors stub), 8-K 0001546417-25-000101 (Q1 2025 earnings press release EX-99.1), 8-K 0001546417-25-000076 (Item 5.07 shareholder vote).

Skipped (6): (1) entire 8-K 0001546417-25-000076 — shareholder-vote item with no substantive content; (2) 10-Q Risk Factors — 'no material changes' boilerplate, nothing nameable; (3) 'choppy macro environment' — bare macro category, banned; (4) 'reaffirming our full-year financial guidance' — no specific metric named, too vague to coin beyond the Q2 outlook drivers already captured; (5) restaurant openings as a standalone driver — 'opening' is a banned state word; the footprint effect is captured under restaurant_closures (net impact of closures and openings); (6) forward-looking-statements boilerplate risk list (consumer confidence, unemployment, pandemics, etc.) — generic enumerations, not source-attributed causes.

eps_surprise/revenue_surprise: release reports results vs prior year (no explicit consensus), but headline Q1 results in an Item 2.02 8-K are the standard surprise-driver evidence per the earnings convention.
Brand-level comparable sales coined per ontology R9 (brand metric = own driver, separate from company-wide same_store_sales).
10-Q is part 2 of 2 — names coined only from the visible part; other parts handled by other bots.
